---
title: "figure5_network"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "11/18/2024"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggraph)
library(igraph)
library(Hmisc)
library(writexl)

library(tidygraph)

```


#demographic
```{r}
food_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQA862.xlsx")
nutrition_data = read_xlsx("data/FFQB800.xlsx")
species = read_xlsx("data/species_866.xlsx")
metadata = read_xlsx("data/ethnicity_866.xlsx")

food_data <- food_data %>%
  mutate(across(c("Mushrooms", "Papaya", "Orange", "Chapati"), as.numeric))
food_data
nutrition_data
species
metadata
```

```{r}
colnames(species)[1] = "Participant_ID"
colnames(metadata)[1] = "Participant_ID"
threshold <- nrow(species) * 0.5

species_filtered1 <- species %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > nrow(species) * 0.2)
species_filtered1
species_filtered2 <- species %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x > 0) > threshold)

#abundance_threshold <- 0.0001
#species_filtered2 <- species_filtered %>% select_if(function(x) sum(x) > (abundance_threshold * sum(species)))
#species_filtered2
species_filtered2 <- species_filtered2[, !names(species_filtered2) %in% c("unclassified", "others", "Homo sapiens")]
#species_filtered2$`Homo sapiens`
#species_filtered2
food_data[is.na(food_data)] <- 0
#food_data <- data.frame(lapply(food_data, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))
nutrition_data[is.na(nutrition_data)] <- 0
```


```{r}
food_data
nutrition_data
species_filtered2
species_filtered1
metadata
```


## knowledge graph


```{r}
create_microbiome_knowledge_graph <- function(food_data, nutrition_data, 
                                            species_data, metadata,
                                            species_threshold = 0.3) {  # Add threshold parameter
  # Ensure all datasets have the same participants and in the same order
  common_participants <- Reduce(intersect, list(
    food_data$Participant_ID,
    nutrition_data$Participant_ID,
    species_data$Participant_ID
  ))
  
  # Filter and arrange all datasets
  food_data <- food_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  nutrition_data <- nutrition_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  species_data <- species_data %>%
    filter(Participant_ID %in% common_participants) %>%
    arrange(Participant_ID)
  
  # Get categories
  food_categories <- setdiff(colnames(food_data), "Participant_ID")
  nutrition_categories <- setdiff(colnames(nutrition_data), "Participant_ID")
  species_categories <- setdiff(colnames(species_data), "Participant_ID")
  
  # Calculate correlations with p-values using rcorr
  calculate_correlations <- function(data1, categories1, data2, categories2) {
    mat1 <- as.matrix(data1[, categories1])
    mat2 <- as.matrix(data2[, categories2])
    
    complete_cases <- complete.cases(mat1, mat2)
    if(!all(complete_cases)) {
      mat1 <- mat1[complete_cases, ]
      mat2 <- mat2[complete_cases, ]
    }
    
    rcorr_result <- rcorr(cbind(mat1, mat2), type = "pearson")
    
    n1 <- ncol(mat1)
    n2 <- ncol(mat2)
    cors <- rcorr_result$r[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    p_values <- rcorr_result$P[1:n1, (n1+1):(n1+n2)]
    
    rownames(cors) <- categories1
    colnames(cors) <- categories2
    rownames(p_values) <- categories1
    colnames(p_values) <- categories2
    
    return(list(correlations = cors, p_values = p_values))
  }
  
  species_food_results <- calculate_correlations(
    species_data, species_categories,
    food_data, food_categories
  )
  
  species_nutrition_results <- calculate_correlations(
    species_data, species_categories,
    nutrition_data, nutrition_categories
  )
  
  # Create edges from correlations
  create_correlation_edges <- function(cor_results, relationship_type) {
    cors <- cor_results$correlations
    p_values <- cor_results$p_values
    edges <- which(p_values <= 0.01 & abs(cors) >= species_threshold, arr.ind = TRUE)  # Add threshold filter
    
    if(nrow(edges) > 0) {
      tibble(
        from = rownames(cors)[edges[,1]],
        to = colnames(cors)[edges[,2]],
        relationship = relationship_type,
        weight = cors[cbind(edges[,1], edges[,2])],
        p_value = p_values[cbind(edges[,1], edges[,2])]
      )
    } else {
      tibble(from = character(), to = character(), 
             relationship = character(), weight = numeric(),
             p_value = numeric())
    }
  }
  
  # Get species that have significant strong connections
  species_with_connections <- unique(c(
    rownames(species_food_results$correlations)[rowSums(species_food_results$p_values <= 0.01 & 
                                                        abs(species_food_results$correlations) >= species_threshold) > 0],
    rownames(species_nutrition_results$correlations)[rowSums(species_nutrition_results$p_values <= 0.01 & 
                                                             abs(species_nutrition_results$correlations) >= species_threshold) > 0]
  ))
  
  # Create nodes with custom colors
  ethnicity_nodes <- tibble(
    id = c("C", "I", "M"),
    type = "ethnicity",
    group = "ethnicity",
    color = "darkviolet"  # Gray
  )
  
  food_nodes <- tibble(
    id = food_categories,
    type = "food",
    group = "food",
    color = "#FF6B6B"  # Coral red
  )
  
  nutrition_nodes <- tibble(
    id = nutrition_categories,
    type = "nutrition", 
    group = "nutrition",
    color = "#4ECDC4"  # Turquoise
  )
  
  species_nodes <- tibble(
    id = species_with_connections,
    type = "species",
    group = "species",
    color = "#95A5A6"  # Light gray
  )
  
  # Combine nodes
  nodes <- bind_rows(
    ethnicity_nodes,
    food_nodes,
    nutrition_nodes,
    species_nodes
  )
  
  # Create edges
  species_food_edges <- create_correlation_edges(species_food_results, "species_food")
  species_nutrition_edges <- create_correlation_edges(species_nutrition_results, "species_nutrition")
  
  edges <- bind_rows(
    species_food_edges,
    species_nutrition_edges
  ) %>%
    mutate(correlation_direction = ifelse(weight > 0, "positive", "negative"))
  
  # Create graph
  graph <- tbl_graph(nodes = nodes, edges = edges, directed = FALSE) %>%
    activate(edges) %>%
    mutate(abs_weight = abs(weight))
  
  # Create visualization with custom node colors
  p <- ggraph(graph, layout = "fr", weights = abs_weight) +
    geom_edge_link(aes(color = correlation_direction, 
                      width = abs(weight),
                      alpha = abs(weight))) +
    geom_node_point(aes(color = color), size = 4) +
    geom_node_text(aes(label = id, 
                   size = group),  # Map size to node group
               repel = TRUE) +
    scale_size_manual(values = c(
      "food" = 6.5,        # Larger font for food nodes
      "nutrition" = 6.5,   # Medium font for nutrition nodes
      "species" = 4,     # Smaller font for species nodes
      "ethnicity" = 6.5    # Larger font for ethnicity nodes
      )) +
    scale_edge_width_continuous(range = c(0.2, 2)) +
    scale_edge_color_manual(values = c("positive" = "salmon2", "negative" = "steelblue2")) +
    scale_edge_alpha(range = c(0.2, 0.8)) +
    scale_color_identity() +  # Use the actual colors specified in nodes
    theme_graph(base_family = "sans") +
    labs(title = "Microbiome Knowledge Graph",
         subtitle = paste("Significant connections (p < 0.05, |correlation| >=", species_threshold, ") between\n",
                         length(species_with_connections), 
                         "species and food/nutrition variables\n",
                         "Edge width represents correlation strength\n",
                         "Red = positive correlation, Blue = negative correlation")) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.box = "vertical",
      plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20)
    )
  
  return(list(
    graph = graph,
    plot = p,
    nodes = nodes,
    edges = edges,
    species_with_connections = species_with_connections,
    results = list(
      species_food = species_food_results,
      species_nutrition = species_nutrition_results
    )
  ))
}
```


```{r fig.width=14, fig.height=14}
# result <- create_microbiome_knowledge_graph(food_data, nutrition_data, 
#                                           species_filtered1, metadata)

result = create_microbiome_knowledge_graph(food_data, nutrition_data, 
                                                species_filtered1, metadata,
                                                species_threshold = 0.15)
# View the plot
print(result$plot)

# Save the plot
#ggsave("figure/microbiome_graph3.pdf", result$plot, width = 24, height = 20)

# Check correlation summary
cat("\nNumber of significant correlations:", nrow(result$edges))
cat("\nCorrelation strength summary:\n")
print(summary(abs(result$edges$weight)))
```
